name: archer pull up
instructions: Start by hanging from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back.
As you pull yourself up, bend one arm and bring your elbow towards your side, while keeping the other arm straight.
Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar and your bent arm is fully flexed.
Lower yourself back down with control, straightening the bent arm and repeating the movement on the other side.

Start by hanging from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Engage your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back. As you pull yourself up, bend one arm and bring your elbow towards your side, while keeping the other arm straight. Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar and your bent arm is fully flexed. Lower yourself back down with control, straightening the bent arm and repeating the movement on the other side. Alternate sides with each repetition.

conditionNotes.note: Favor symmetrical loading and supported variations if fatigue changes your posture.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Avoid loaded spinal flexion and excessive hinge depth. Brace core and use supported positions.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
